chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يُسْتَحَبُّ مِنَ الْهَدْيِ وَ مَا يَجُوزُ مِنْهُ وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 181 – What is recommended from the sacrificial animal, and what is allowed from it, and what is not allowed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/167)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/167)
text: حَجَجْتُ بِأَهْلِي سَنَةً فَعَزَّتِ الْأَضَاحِيُّ فَانْطَلَقْتُ فَاشْتَرَيْتُ شَاتَيْنِ بِغَلَاءٍ فَلَمَّا أَلْقَيْتُ إِهَابَهُمَا نَدِمْتُ نَدَامَةً شَدِيدَةً لِمَا رَأَيْتُ بِهِمَا مِنَ الْهُزَالِ فَأَتَيْتُهُ فَأَخْبَرْتُهُ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ عَلَى كُلْيَتَيْهِمَا شَيْ‏ءٌ مِنَ الشَّحْمِ أَجْزَأَتَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Isa reported it, from Yaseen Al Zareyr, from Hareyz, from Al Fuzayl who said,

‘I performed Hajj with my family one year, and the sacrificial animals were expensive. So I went and bought two sheep for a high price. So, when I examined their cuticles, I regretted it with an intense regret due to what I saw with them from the thinness. So I went over to him<sup>asws</sup> and informed him<sup>asws</sup> of that. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If there was something from the fat upon their kidneys, they would both suffice’.
en.sarwar: 16. Muhammad ibn ‘Isa has narrated from Yasin al-Darir from Hariz from al-Fudayl who has said the following: “One year my family and I went for al-Hajj but cattle became very expensive. I went and bought two sheep, which were expensive. When I examined them closely, I regretted greatly because they were very weak. I brought them and informed him (the Imam) ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about it and he (the Imam) said, ‘If there is any fat on their kidneys, then it is sufficient.’”
